title: Ram Katha 08: HG Amogh Lila Prabhu
summary: Amogh Lila Prabhu, also known as Amogh Lila Das, is a well-known spiritual leader, motivational speaker, lifestyle coach, and engineer. He has served as the vice president of the ISKCON Temple in Dwarka, Delhi. His speeches, often infused with humor and insight, have inspired many to pursue a spiritually enriched life.Originally named Ashish Arora, he was born in Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh, and earned a B.Tech in Computer Science from Delhi College of Engineering. After a successful career in the corporate world, he chose the path of monastic life as a Hare Krishna Brahmachari.He has also built a strong presence on social media, where his spiritual and motivational content continues to resonate with a wide audience. title: Shining Light on the Shadows Within: Carl Jung Meets the Wisdom of Bhakti
summary: Misunderstood warriors, falsely accused maidens, hardhearted mystic yogis, remorseful sages, sex-addicted kingsвАФ The stories of the Ninth Canto of the BhƒБgavatam reflect not just historical characters, but the shadows we all carry within.  At the cusp of entering the climactic Tenth Canto, Raghunath and Kaustubha review the string of stories leading up to it. title: Gospel | Chapter 3: Aug 5, 1882 (Part 4) | Swami Sarvapriyananda
summary: Swami Sarvapriyananda reads and discusses the Gospel of Sri Ramakrishna. The Gospel of Sri Ramakrishna is an English translation of the Bengali spiritual text Sri Sri Ramakrishna Kathamrita by Swami Nikhilananda. The text records conversations of Ramakrishna with his disciples, devotees and visitors, recorded by Mahendranath Gupta, who wrote the book under the pseudonym of "M." This session was recorded on Jun 15, 2021.

title: Darkness, Evil & Satan: A Bhakti Yoga Perspective / Q & A Volume 275
summary: What is Evil? Different tradition have different ideas. In this compelling Q & A episode of Wisdom of the Sages, Raghunath and Kaustubha explore how the Bhakti tradition understands darkness, Satan, and the concept of evil. Is it a cosmic force? A personal being? Or simply the absence of divine light?  Other questions include how to navigate workplace obligations at bars while maintaining spiritual sobriety, and what pure devotional service looks like in todayвАЩs world. title: Feed the Dogs, Love the World: Bhakti YogaвАЩs Expanding Circle of Compassion
summary: Indian hospitality, the soft heart of a mother, and the Dog-Man of Tompkins Square Park all intersect in this powerful exploration of yoga. Drawing from the Maha Upanishad and the insight of Albert Schweitzer, Raghunath and Kaustubha unpack a central bhakti truth: that genuine love of God expresses itself as compassion for all living beingsвАФnot just the ones who vote like you, think like you, or smell like you.  At the heart of this episode is the unforgettable tale of King Rantideva from the ≈ЪrƒЂmad BhƒБgavatamвАФa king whose radical acts of generosity and deep spiritual faith push us to reconsider what it really means to live a spiritual life.  рЯМА Key Highlights:  вАҐвБ† ¬†вБ†вАЬVasudhaiva KutumbakamвАЭ вАФ The Maha UpanishadвАЩs timeless call to expand the borders of your compassion  вАҐвБ† ¬†вБ†How great spiritualists experience sufferingвАФand transform it into compassion  вАҐвБ† ¬†вБ†The real yoga test: when your food is hot, your stomach is empty, and a stranger knocks on the door (with dogs)  вАҐвБ† ¬†вБ†Why bhakti doesnвАЩt exist without compassion  рЯСБпЄПвАНрЯЧ®пЄП Real peace begins when we expand the circle. title: Where Your Mind Goes, You Go: Bhakti Yoga, Thought Power & the Inner Algorithm
summary: Your mind is like an algorithm. Click on lust, fear, or resentmentвАФand guess what shows up on your mental feed? In this episode, Raghunath and Kaustubha explore Yoga is a way to reset the algorithm, and explore the mind as both a garden and a walled city with guarded gates.  Drawing from James AllenвАЩs classic As a Man Thinketh and the final verses of King YayƒБtiвАЩs transformation in the ≈ЪrƒЂmad BhƒБgavatam, they unpack how thought is causal, attention is destiny, and bhakti is the ultimate reprogramming of the heart.    рЯФС Key Highlights:  вАҐвБ† ¬†вБ†James Allen meets the Bhagavatam: вАЬPlant good seeds or get weeds. title: Exiting the Path of Emptiness: A Bhakti Yoga Take on Desire & Liberation
summary: Ancient yoga texts call it sama-darshanaвАФequal vision. ItвАЩs the ability to see the soul in every being, regardless of their body, background, or even behavior. And according to the sages, itвАЩs not just a lofty ideaвАФitвАЩs the very foundation of a life well-lived.  HereвАЩs the twist: itвАЩs not just the moral thing to doвАФitвАЩs the key to happiness, peace of mind, and, ultimately, a graceful exit from this world. In contrast, the pursuit of material fulfillment becomes a path of emptiness.  Listen in as Raghunath and Kaustubha reflect on the yogiвАЩs mindset: to live in a way that places no one else in difficulty, and to love in a way that clears the heart of all fear and resentment.  Key Highlights:  вАҐвБ† ¬†вБ†Sama-darshanaвАФthe yogic practice of seeing beyond externals  вАҐвБ† ¬†вБ†Why a life of non-harm and kindness clears the path to liberation  вАҐвБ† ¬†вБ†вАЬMy name is King Yayati, and IвАЩm a sex addict.вАЭ  вАҐвБ† ¬†вБ†How material desire drives us to harm even those we love  вАҐвБ† ¬†вБ†How spiritual vision helps us let goвАФwithout fear or bitterness  вАҐвБ† ¬†вБ†And how chasing sense gratification will make a goat out of us. рЯРР (and not the good kind of GOAT)  This episode is a call to shift how we see the worldвАФand how we live in it.  рЯОІ Listen now and join the global conversation on living with deeper vision.  #EqualVision #BhaktiYoga #Samadarshana #SrimadBhagavatam #LettingGo #YogaWisdom #WisdomOfTheSages  SB 9.19.12-15   *********************************************************************  LOVE THE PODCAST? title: Seeing the Unseen: Yoga's Call to Look Deeper
summary: Politics shift, bodies age, relationships breakвАФwe see the surface, the temporary illusion. Yogis look deeper to see the eternal reality behind it all.  In this episode, Raghunath and Kaustubha explore how sacred textsвАФthe Bhagavad GƒЂtƒБ, ≈ЪrƒЂmad BhƒБgavatam, the Rig Veda, and the New TestamentвАФurge us to fix our vision on the eternal. From pearls resting on a hidden thread to the saintsвАЩ constant gaze toward the divine, these traditions all point to the same truth: whatвАЩs real canвАЩt always be seen with the eyes - so look deeper though the eyes of knowledge.¬†  Key Highlights:  вАҐвБ† ¬†вБ†Pearls and cloudsвАФwhat the sages say about what really holds life together  вАҐвБ† ¬†вБ†The world is a stereogram - keep staring into it and eventually the vision pops  вАҐвБ† ¬†вБ†KaustubhaвАЩs near bike-lane road rage (he almost lost it.)  вАҐвБ† ¬†вБ†How bhakti can awaken even in a materially entangled life (yes, thereвАЩs hope for all of us)  рЯФФ Subscribe for daily episodes and timeless inspiration on the path of bhakti yoga.  #BhaktiYoga #YogaPhilosophy #SrimadBhagavatam #BhagavadGita #RigVeda #StPaul #SpiritualVision #SeeTheUnseen  SB 9.18.47-51   *********************************************************************  LOVE THE PODCAST? title: Episode 20
summary: Sri Brihad-bhagavatamrita¬†is an epic work originally composed in Sanskrit during the sixteenth century by¬†вБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†Sanatana GoswamiвБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†, one of¬†вБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†Chaitanya MahaprabhuвБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†'s leading disciples. It is one of the first books written by any of the¬†вБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†Six Goswamis of Vrindavan.вБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†In three volumes, it gives an engaging account of two great spiritual quests. In one, the Vedic sage Narada searches throughout the universe for Krishna's greatest devotee. In the other, the cowherd boy Gopa Kumar searches for¬†вБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†Krishna HimselfвБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†вАФthe Supreme Person, the ultimate goal of life.The¬†Bhagavatamrita¬†is rooted in ancient history. It is the appendix to the sage Jaimini Rishi's version of the historical saga¬†Mahabharata, and was spoken by the hero of the¬†вБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†Srimad-BhagavatamвБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†, King Pariksit, to his mother, just moments before the king's death.This edition of¬†Sri Brihad-bhagavatamrita¬†is the work of Gopiparanadhana Dasa, who was part of the team of¬†вБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†Srila Prabhupada'sвБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†¬†scholarly disciples who completed the translation and commentary on Srila Prabhupada's life's workвАФthe Srimad-BhagavatamвАФafter Srila Prabhupada's passing in 1977. Gopiparanadhana Dasa wrote several volumes of translation and commentary on the Goswamis' writings before his passing in 2011.

title: Gospel | Chapter 3: Aug 5, 1882 (Part 3) | Swami Sarvapriyananda
summary: Swami Sarvapriyananda reads and discusses the Gospel of Sri Ramakrishna. The Gospel of Sri Ramakrishna is an English translation of the Bengali spiritual text Sri Sri Ramakrishna Kathamrita by Swami Nikhilananda. The text records conversations of Ramakrishna with his disciples, devotees and visitors, recorded by Mahendranath Gupta, who wrote the book under the pseudonym of "M." This session was recorded on Jun 8, 2021.

title: Episode 503: Chapter 12, verses 7,8 and 9
summary: Bhagavad Gita Ch. 12 вАЬYoga of¬†DevotionвАЭ Verses 7, 8, & 9 The lecture discusses KrishnaвАЩs assurance that he will rescue his devotees from the ocean of ever-changing samsara, fraught with death and distraction. He offers¬† Abhyasa Yoga as an alternative for those who cannot fix their minds on the divine. title: Loss. Confusion. Melancholy. Then Freedom. | Q&A Volume 274
summary: With humor, vulnerability, and sharp philosophical insight, Raghunath and Kaustubha unpack the gritty reality of spiritual growth: that it sometimes begins with a period of loss, confusion, or even melancholyвАФbut on the other side is joy, clarity, and freedom.  This Q & A episode tackles big, relatable questions from listeners on the spiritual path:¬†  вАҐвБ† ¬†вБ†How do I live a life of dharmaвАФseeing the good in everyoneвАФwithout letting people take advantage of me or drain my energy?¬†  вАҐвБ† ¬†вБ†How do I practice detachment without becoming emotionally distant from the people I love?  вАҐвБ† ¬†вБ†Does devotion to Krishna mean not exploring other spiritual traditions?  Discover how the simple mantra вАЬAdjust. Tolerate. Focus.вАЭ can become a compass when life feels chaotic and the heart is pulled in too many directions.  Key Highlights:  вАҐвБ† ¬†вБ†Adjusting your circumstances vs. tolerating themвАФand how to know which to do  вАҐвБ† ¬†вБ†Why KrishnaвАЩs blessings can feel like lemon in milkвАФand why thatвАЩs a good thing  вАҐвБ† ¬†вБ†The difference between real detachment and just shutting down emotionally  вАҐвБ† ¬†вБ†A perspective-shifting metaphor about butter thatвАЩll stick with you forever  вАҐвБ† ¬†вБ†Why kirtan and philosophy together are like spiritual ayahuasca (minus the vomiting)  Whether youвАЩre navigating toxic coworkers or simply trying to love your family without losing yourself, this episode will give you both tools and comfort for the inner work that really matters.  рЯОІ Listen now and join thousands on the transformative path of bhakti.  рЯМЯ Subscribe for daily wisdom and spiritual guidance with Raghunath & Kaustubha.  #BhaktiYoga #SpiritualGrowth #DetachmentWithLove #KrishnaConsciousness #WisdomOfTheSages #SrimadBhagavatam #KirtanAndPhilosophy #LetGoAndLetKrishna #AdjustTolerateFocus   *********************************************************************  LOVE THE PODCAST?  WE ARE COMMUNITY SUPPORTED AND WOULD LOVE FOR YOU TO JOIN! title: Desire, Folly, Regret & Ultimately....Transformation
summary: Raghunath and Kaustubha dive into the comical yet piercingly honest downfall of King YayƒБti and explore how the ancient Vedic culture didnвАЩt just shame desireвАФit organized it, regulated it, and ultimately helped one transcended it. Drawing from the ≈ЪrƒЂmad-BhƒБgavatam, we uncover how real evolution begins not by denying our lower nature, but by accepting the guidance of wisdom tradition that helps us see through it.  Key Highlights:  вАҐвБ† ¬†вБ†Why the world makes you foolish when you chase desire  The brilliance of varбєЗƒБ≈Ыrama: not a box, but a ladder  Why trying to be Krishna never works  вАҐвБ† ¬†вБ†Young PuruвАЩs ultimate mic drop moment of detachment and devotion  вАҐвБ† ¬†вБ†Plus: How a hardcore cookbook made its way onto the show (Walk-On WednesdayвА¶ on a Friday)  вАҐвБ† ¬†вБ†And, RaghunathвАЩs high school brawl over a mustached senior (seriously)  рЯОІ Listen in and laugh your way through lust, curses, wisdom, and the yogic path of real freedom.  рЯМЯ Subscribe for daily spiritual insights, and join thousands exploring bhakti-yoga through sacred texts and honest conversation.  #SrimadBhagavatam #BhaktiYoga #KingYayati #SpiritualGrowth #DesireAndDetachment #WisdomOfTheSages #VedicWisdom #YogicLife  SB 9.18.30-47  Find Laura Lee Flanagan's cookbook, Hardcore Vegetarian at https://a.co/d/8WgdWcn   *********************************************************************  LOVE THE PODCAST? title: Hindu at Heart: Psychotherapist & HAF Board Member Dr. Kavita Pallod Sekhsaria
summary: In this episode, part of Indu ViswanathanвАЩs Hindu at Heart series, she talks with psychotherapist and HAF Board Member Dr. Kavita Pallod Sekhsaria. They discuss KavitaвАЩs life growing up Hindu in Texas in the 1980s and 90s, her path into psychotherapy, some of the issues related to this specific to the Hindu American community, as well as some of the challenges sheвАЩs seen as the oppressor versus oppressed worldview now dominates the field and professional associations of psychology. Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: A story about cultural appropriation and open-mindedness
summary: If this collection of practices, teachings, philosophies, and methodologies for living, for spiritual exploration that we call Hinduism, that we call the Hindu Dharma Traditions, has real value in the world for people who may never fully embrace it or call themselves Hindu вАФ and it certainly does, of that I am entirely convinced вАФ then I think we must be careful to be as welcoming of people dipping their toes in the water as I was welcomed initially. Missed the first series of episodes of All About Hinduism? Start here on Apple Podcasts or Spotify.  Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Ask Swami with Swami Sarvapriyananda | Mar 23rd, 2025
summary: 27,697 views  Apr 15, 2025  AskSwami Q&A | Swami Sarvapriyananda Q&A session led by Swami Sarvapriyananda at the Vedanta Society of New York on Mar 23rd, 2025. List of questions with timestamps: 00:00 - Intro 01:43 - Explain the two ways of approaching non-duality: вАЬI am all of itвАЭ vs вАЬI am none of itвАЭ? 15:24 - Is devotion (bhakti) merely an aid to knowledge in Advaita, or something more? 29:58 - Is the intense love of God (parabhakti) a form of insanity or mysticism? 36:16 - Is Advaita Vedanta falsifiable? 52:06 - The importance of asking questions in the study of Vedanta. 58:44 - Should we always practice unconditional love? 01:11:14 - What is the real meaning of вАШjadaвАЩ (insentient) in Advaita? 01:20:30 - What is the relationship between Maya and Chit (consciousness)?

title: Ep. 276 вАУ Approaching the Mystery
summary: In this exploration of the fear of suffering as we get older, Ram Dass talks about approaching the mystery of aging and death in the same adventurous manner as his friend Timothy Leary. For more on the relationship between Ram Dass and Timothy Leary, please check out Dying to Know, a book that details their epic friendship and TimothyвАЩs process of dying. The Ram Dass community gathers regularly to engage in meaningful discussions about the podcast. We invite you to join us and share your curiosities, insights, and wisdom. Sign up for the General Fellowship to receive event invitations directly in your inbox. This talk from June 1996 is part of an aging study group Ram Dass conducted to help guide his book on aging, Still Here. Fear of future suffering is a major issue for all us, but especially when it comes to aging. Ram Dass explores how we tend to respond to that fear with massive denial, and how we can work on coming to terms with those fears through a shift in perspective.Ram Dass talks about how his work with people who are severely ill or in the process of dying has helped prepare him for his own death. He talks about the fear of losing oneвАЩs analytic, linear mind, which is so valued in this culture, and how we can open ourselves up to the value of non-linear thinking.Finally, Ram Dass details the time he spent with his old friend Timothy Leary as he approached his death. For Timothy, death was another glorious adventure. Ram Dass wonders if we shouldnвАЩt all add a touch of the вАЬLeary MethodвАЭ as a way to approach the mystery of aging and dying.Sponsors of this Episode: Ram Dass Here & Now is brought to you by¬†BetterHelp. Give online therapy a try at¬†betterhelp.com/ramdass¬†and get on your way to being your best self. This show is also sponsored by Magic Mind, a matcha-based energy shot infused with nootropics and adaptogens designed to crush procrastination, brain fog, & fatigue. You have a limited offer you can use now, that gets you up to 48% off your first subscription or 20% off one-time purchases with the code RAMDASS at www.magicmind.com/ramdass. Reunion¬†is offering¬†$250¬†off any stay to the Love, Serve, Remember community. Simply use the code вАЬBeHere250вАЭ when booking. Disconnect from the world so you can reconnect with yourself at Reunion.¬†¬†Hotel |¬†www.reunionhotelandwellness.com¬†Retreats |¬†www.reunionexperience.org вАЬWhat Timothy added was the fun of it, the adventure as you approach the mystery. When youвАЩre approaching a mystery, what space do you want to be in in your head? Getting free of guilt? I mean, is that the one you want? Which one do you want? Which one opens to the possibilities? And Timothy was an adventurer, I mean, as I can tell you from being in the backwash for years. Timothy took me fighting and screaming into adventure. He opened up the whole image of dying into the possibility that it was a celebratory, adventurous, exciting part of life.вАЭ вАУ Ram Dass See Privacy Policy at https://art19.com/privacy and California Privacy Notice at https://art19.com/privacy#do-not-sell-my-info.

title: PakistanвАЩs support for terrorism wonвАЩt stop until they are held accountable | Michael Rubin
summary: In this episode Samir Kalra speaks with Michael Rubin about the parallels between HamasвАЩ attack on Israel on 10/7/23 and the most recent terrorist attack in Pahalgam, Kashmir; how PakistanвАЩs ISI and support for terror groups is an issue for Americans as well as Indians; what the US can do in support of India in their fight against terrorist groups, including why President Trump needs to make a visit to Kashmir to show the strength of US support for India;¬† why it might be time for the international community to show some вАЬtough loveвАЭ to Pakistan; and how both China and Pakistan are trying to promote separatism within India. Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.
